title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

text_content: The primary actor for maintaining the operation of the equipment is the Hospital Technician: The Hospital Technician is trained and/or certified individual responsible for maintaining the operation of the hardware. Typically, the Hospital Technician is the first line of support before contacting the Customer Support Representative and /or will work with the Customer Support Representative to help resolve hardware issues.  The Hospital Technician would be expected to attend a training program provided by a vendor or will obtain on the job training during installation and servicing. The Hospital Technician is not responsible for maintaining clinical correctness of the hardware, only the operation of the hardware. source: D58308 HAL 4.0 Device Description Rev 1.0.docx.pdf
title: 1.0 Executive Summary > 1.2 Device Classification > 1.3.6 Operator Profiles (Continued)
text_content: The hospital technician, typically a biomedical engineer, is a local, in-clinic resource that is responsible for maintaining the treatment delivery system and associated software and hardware. This resource is trained to a defined level of competency and is provided with limited-service access to the system. The role of hospital technician is to maintain operation of the treatment delivery system and is the first line of support before contacting the Varian Customer Support Representative and /or will work with the Varian Customer Support Representative to help resolve hardware issues. The system is an assortment of components which can be a combination of hardware and software. When preparing hardware for clinical use, the hospital technician may calibrate the hardware and ensure the accuracy of the hardware parameters. Americas: Physicist, Dosimetrist, Radiation Therapist APAC: Physicist, Radiation Physicist, Medical Physicist EMEA: Physicist

title: 1.0 Executive Summary > 1.2 Device Classification > 1.3.6 Operator Profiles
text_content: HAL is operated by persons who have the qualifications, licensure, and/or certification as defined by their regional authorities.  These persons are typically referred to as Radiation Therapists (RTs). They operate the device following the treatment prescription, and under the general supervision of the Radiation Oncologist (RO), or other required person as defined by regional authority.  Medical linear accelerator quality assurance is the responsibility of persons as defined by regional authority and is typically the responsibility of the medical physicist. Service is provided by the manufacturer or by a qualified local in-house service resource. The information in this section outlines the roles that are typical in the Radiation Oncology clinic and the titles that are typically assigned to that role. This UAP serves as a communication vehicle for the Halcyon 2.0 project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. Writers are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up-to-date throughout the project and modified as necessary. If the key milestones remain unchanged, then details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. text_content: The actor who maintains responsibility for the Quality Assurance Program is the Quality Assurance Authority: A healthcare worker who actively takes part in the Quality Assurance program in a radiation oncology department. The Quality Assurance program encompasses but is not limited to the daily, weekly, monthly and annual QA of the radiation therapy device, the QA of the treatment planning system and treatment plans, the QA of simulators, the QA of CT scanners and other imaging devices, the QA of measurement equipment and the QA of clinical aspects (peer review, new patient planning conference, chart review and film review). The Quality Assurance Authority defines the QA/QC processes, the tests and specifications and assigns the responsibilities. This individual is also responsible for analyzing the data and signing off on the test. title: 1. Purpose

source: D93257 Halcyon Evolve User Assistance Plan Rev02.docx.pdf
title: 1. Introduction > 1.2 Purpose and Scope of the UAP
text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. Serve as a communication vehicle for the project team and a repository of information for UA tasks. User Assistance Developers (UADs) are part of the project team, attend project meetings, and report status regularly to the project team. The UAP is intended to be kept up to date throughout the project and updated as needed. If key milestones remain unchanged, details in the project plan can be updated without requiring reapproval of this document. Scope of UA updates is set by the trigger for the release (section 1.1).  UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. source: D78333 Ethos 4.0 Description of interoperability Study Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 1. Basic Information > 2.2 Interoperation Maintenance
text_content: Support & Maintenance Process Customers and any employee that experience erroneous behavior are advised to report these errors to the business unit that has sold the TPS system. These reports are analyzed by the respective complaint handling department. Every error is inserted into the corrective and preventive action (CAPA) and complaint handling procedures and, where necessary, is reproduced in the laboratory for analysis. The complaint handling and the CAPA procedures ensure that the complaints are assessed for the need of Medical Device Reporting (MDR). Also ensured are a root cause analysis of the erroneous behavior and the provision of appropriate change requests for fixing the error. Depending on the significance of the problem, required changes are made by returning to the appropriate development state and implementing appropriate fixes. The changes are then retested and re-evaluated and are either distributed as updates through standard service

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 4.0 Requirements for Risk Management Activities and their Review (Continued)
text_content: FMEA sessions require a cross-functional and technically biased team approach. FMEA analysis relies on domain knowledge of the team to identify components to consider and specific manner or way by which a failure occurs.  Analysis is conducted thru brainstorming sessions by the team.   Manner or way by which a failure occurs is highly domain (mechanical, electrical, or software) specific. Process FMEA investigates the risk in manufacturing and the supply chain that may cause material or product not meeting requirements, manufacturing operation impact or manufacturing operator impact. Contributing documents such as specifications, requirements, reports, investigations, and studies shall be reviewed by persons with recorded competence in the documents’ subjects. Where relevant, these persons shall have knowledge and experience with the device(s) in this RMP’s scope as well as familiarity with the device’s use(s) and technologies.

PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. title: 5. Detailed Project Scope > 5.1 Task and Effort Estimates
text_content: Effort estimates for main activities will be updated as work proceeds and requirements are clarified. Estimates are by site and in workdays over the life of the project. The following table summarizes estimates on all activities to author and publish UA. Activities comprise: Feature or Scope items – Authoring and updating content for specific program features. General content – Maintaining UA (updates for corrections or changes from the prior release) and updating overall content not specific to features. CRNs – Formatting, finalizing content for publication, and publishing. Quality assurance and production – Reviews, including formal and technical (DITA XML QA) reviews, and revisions per review findings. Management and localization – Administration, meetings, coordination with GPL Localization on translation work, distribution.
